oligos.name: TruSeq universal adapter
oligos.role: P5 forked-adapter oligo (top/universal strand of the Y-shaped adapter); contributes the Read 1 sequencing primer binding site and P5 flow-cell sequence
oligos.notes: Printed as AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATC*T; the * marks a phosphorothioate linkage between the last two bases (C*T) to protect the terminal T overhang from nuclease cleavage. Cytosines are methyl-modified (premethylated) to survive bisulfite conversion. Anneals to the indexed adapter over the 12-bp AGATCGGAAGAGC stem, leaving a single 3'-T overhang for TA ligation.

oligos.name: TruSeq indexed adapter (index 1–12)
oligos.role: P7 forked-adapter oligo (bottom/index strand of the Y-shaped adapter); carries the 6-nt i7 sample index and the P7 flow-cell sequence
oligos.notes: 5'-terminal phosphorylated for ligation; cytosines premethylated for bisulfite protection. 6-nt index sits between ...CCAGTCAC and ATCTCGTAT... The 12 index sequences from Table 1 (5'->3'): idx1 ATCACG, idx2 CGATGT, idx3 TTAGGC, idx4 TGACCA, idx5 ACAGTG, idx6 GCCAAT, idx7 CAGATC, idx8 ACTTGA, idx9 GATCAG, idx10 TAGCTT, idx11 GGCTAC, idx12 CTTGTA. Example (index 1): GATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCACATCACGATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG.

oligos.name: TruSeq Y-shaped (forked) sequencing adapter
oligos.role: Double-oligo forked adapter ligated to dA-tailed MspI fragments
oligos.notes: The two oligos are complementary over the 12-bp AGATCGGAAGAGC stem and diverge into the P5 and P7 arms, forming a Y. Used at 1:20 dilution in the ligation. All cytosines premethylated so the adapter is not bisulfite-converted.

oligos.name: QP1 (P5 PCR primer)
oligos.role: Forward library-amplification primer for both PCR rounds (P5)
oligos.notes: IDT-synthesized, HPLC-purified; used at 300 nM (round 1) and 500 nM (round 2). Matches the 5' end of the TruSeq universal adapter.

oligos.name: QP2 (P7 PCR primer)
oligos.role: Reverse library-amplification primer for both PCR rounds (P7)
oligos.notes: IDT-synthesized, HPLC-purified; used at 300 nM (round 1) and 500 nM (round 2). Its reverse complement (TCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG) is the 3' end of the indexed adapter.

oligos.name: Read 1 sequencing primer (standard TruSeq)
oligos.role: Primes Read 1 into the bisulfite-converted MspI genomic insert
oligos.notes: Not printed separately; this is the standard Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer sequence, which is embedded verbatim within the TruSeq universal adapter.

oligos.name: Index (i7) sequencing primer (standard TruSeq)
oligos.notes: Standard TruSeq index-read primer, embedded verbatim within the indexed adapter (region immediately 5' of the 6-nt index).

oligos.name: Read 2 sequencing primer (standard TruSeq)
oligos.role: Primes Read 2 (paired end) into the insert from the P7 side
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer; the reverse complement of the indexed-adapter stem region (GATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC). Paired-end 100-bp reads are described in the protocol.

final_library.source_label: scRRBS final library (TruSeq single-index, bisulfite-converted MspI insert)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGA(P5) GATCTACAC TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT(Rd1 primer / MspI-CG start) [CDNA = bisulfite-converted MspI genomic fragment] AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC(Rd2/index primer site) [SAMPLE_INDEX:6](i7) ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG(P7) -3'
annotation_lines: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGA  = P5 flow-cell sequence (QP1 primer binding site)
TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT = Read 1 sequencing primer binding site (ends at the ligation junction / MspI CG remnant)
[CDNA]                = bisulfite-converted MspI genomic insert (begins with filled-in CGG MspI remnant; C->T where unmethylated)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC = adapter stem + Read 2 / index sequencing primer binding site
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG = P7 flow-cell sequence (reverse complement of QP2 primer)

evidence.locator: scRRBS final library (TruSeq single-index, bisulfite-converted MspI insert)

library_generation.title: Single-cell lysis
library_generation.summary: One picked cell is lysed with protease in one tube, releasing naked double-stranded genomic DNA.
library_generation.note: Lysis 3 h at 50 C then 75 C 30 min to inactivate protease; ~60 fg unmethylated lambda-DNA (~1% mass) is spiked in as a bisulfite non-conversion control. No purification is performed after this point until after bisulfite conversion.

library_generation.title: MspI digestion
library_generation.summary: MspI cuts C^CGG, fragmenting the genome and enriching CpG-dense regions, leaving 5'-CG overhangs.
library_generation.note: 9 U MspI, 37 C 3 h, 80 C 20 min inactivation. Methylation-insensitive, so all CCGG sites are cut regardless of 5mC.
library_generation.product:
5'-  CG XXXXXXXXXXXX    -3'
3'-     XXXXXXXXXXXX GC -5'
(each fragment carries 5'-CG overhangs at both ends)

library_generation.title: End-repair / dA-tailing
library_generation.summary: Klenow (exo-) fills in the CG overhangs and adds a single 3'-dA overhang to each end.
library_generation.note: dNTP mix skewed to dATP (40 uM dATP, 4 uM dCTP, 4 uM dGTP): the recessed 3' ends are filled to the CG then a non-templated A is added for TA ligation. 37 C 40 min, 75 C 15 min.
library_generation.product:
5'-  CGXXXXXXXXXXXXCG A -3'
3'- A GCXXXXXXXXXXXXGC   -5'
(blunt filled ends + 3'-A overhang)

library_generation.title: TruSeq adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: Premethylated Y-shaped TruSeq indexed adapters are ligated to the dA-tailed fragments via their T overhang.
library_generation.note: 30 Weiss U T4 DNA ligase, 1:20 diluted adapter, 16 C 30 min then 4 C overnight; 65 C 20 min inactivation. Adapter cytosines are methylated so they resist bisulfite conversion.

library_generation.title: Bisulfite conversion
library_generation.summary: Sodium bisulfite converts unmethylated cytosines in the insert to uracil; methylated Cs and the premethylated adapters are preserved.
library_generation.note: MethylCode kit: 98 C 10 min then 64 C 2.5 h. Converted DNA purified on Zymo spin columns with 10 ng tRNA carrier, eluted in 30 uL.
library_generation.product:
5'- [adapter, unchanged]-[insert with unmethylated C -> U]-[adapter, unchanged] -3'
(single-stranded, uracil-containing insert)

library_generation.title: First-round PCR (Pfu Cx)
library_generation.summary: 25 cycles with QP1/QP2 and uracil-tolerant PfuTurbo Cx polymerase copy uracil as thymine and complete the P5/P7 ends.
library_generation.note: 95 C 2 min; 25x (95 C 20 s, 60 C 30 s, 72 C 1 min); 72 C 5 min. Twice AMPure XP purified. PfuTurbo Cx is resistant to uracil stalling.

library_generation.title: Second-round PCR (Phusion/KAPA)
library_generation.summary: A further 25 cycles with QP1/QP2 enrich the library to sufficient quantity.
library_generation.note: 98 C 2 min; 25x (98 C 10 s, 60 C 30 s, 72 C 1 min); 72 C 5 min. One AMPure XP purification. KAPA HiFi HotStart is an accepted alternative.

library_generation.title: Size selection (200–700 bp)
library_generation.summary: The amplified library is resolved on a 12% native polyacrylamide TBE gel and 200–700 bp fragments are excised, crush-and-soak recovered, and AMPure-cleaned to remove primer dimers (~120 bp).
library_generation.note: Gel-based selection removes the abundant scRRBS adapter/primer dimers. Final library size distribution typically 160–350 bp; yield ~20–30 ng.

library_sequencing.note: Standard TruSeq Read 1 primer (embedded in the P5/universal adapter). Its sequence is identical to the top strand, so it anneals to the bottom strand and reads rightward into the bisulfite-converted MspI genomic insert, starting at the filled-in MspI (CGG) remnant. 100-bp paired-end on HiSeq 2000/2500.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer  5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-3'
                                                 --------> Read 1 (100 nt)
top    5'- ...ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[==BS-converted MspI insert==]AGATCGGAAGAGC... -3'
bottom 3'- ...TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[==   insert complement (RC)  ==]TCTAGCCTTCTCG... -5'

library_sequencing.note: Standard TruSeq i7 index primer; its sequence matches the top strand immediately 5' of the 6-nt index, so it anneals to the bottom strand and reads rightward across the 6-nt sample index (indexes 1-12). Read off the same (bottom) template as Read 1.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer  5'-GATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC-3'
                                                   --------> i7 read (6 nt)
top    5'- ...AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[SAMPLE_INDEX:6]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
bottom 3'- ...TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG[ index compl. ]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'

library_sequencing.note: Standard TruSeq Read 2 primer; its reverse complement is the AGATCGGAAGAGC...CCAGTCAC adapter stem, so the primer anneals to the top strand at that stem and reads leftward (<---) into the bisulfite-converted insert from the P7 side. Paired-end mate of Read 1.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top    5'- ...[==BS-converted MspI insert==]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[i7]... -3'
bottom 3'- ...[==   insert complement (RC)  ==]TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG[i7c]... -5'
              Read 2 (100 nt) <--------  3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG-5'  (Read 2 primer, anneals to top strand)

title: scRRBS
description: Single-cell reduced-representation bisulfite sequencing (scRRBS) profiles genome-wide DNA methylation (5mC + 5hmC) at single-base resolution from a single mammalian cell or a few pooled cells. Genomic DNA is MspI-digested (C^CGG) to enrich CpG-dense regions, end-repaired/dA-tailed, ligated to premethylated Illumina TruSeq indexed adapters, and bisulfite-converted — all in one tube to minimize DNA loss — then PCR-amplified and size-selected. Paired-end reads capture the bisulfite-converted MspI genomic fragments, whose per-CpG methylation is read out digitally as fully methylated or unmethylated.

original_publication.title: Profiling DNA methylome landscapes of mammalian cells with single-cell reduced-representation bisulfite sequencing

authors.name: Hongshan Guo

throughput.summary: Single cell (down to 1 diploid or haploid cell; also 5–100 pooled cells). ~40% of bulk-RRBS CpG sites recovered from one diploid cell (~1 million CpGs; range 0.2–1.5 million). Final library yield ~20–30 ng.
throughput.dna: ~1 million CpG sites/diploid cell (0.2–1.5 million range); ~20–30 ng final library; sequenced to ~10 million 100-bp paired-end reads

publication.statistical_model: Per-CpG methylation level = reported C / (reported C + T) at each genomic position. In single cells (>=3x coverage) each CpG is called digitally as fully methylated (>=90% -> 100%) or unmethylated (<=10% -> 0%); intermediate 10–90% values are discarded. Bisulfite non-conversion rate estimated from non-CG cytosines of the unmethylated lambda-DNA spike-in.

other.label: Original method paper
other.value: Guo et al., Genome Research 23:2126–2135 (2013), doi:10.1101/gr.161679.113 (GEO GSE47343)

other.label: Key innovation
other.value: Single-tube reaction integrating lysis, MspI digestion, end-repair/dA-tailing, adapter ligation and bisulfite conversion with no purification until after conversion

other.label: Mapping efficiency
other.value: ~25% for single cells (plateaus ~50% with more cells)

other.label: Digitized calls
other.value: 91.6% of CpGs in a haploid sperm cell and 86.9% in a diploid mESC are called fully methylated or unmethylated

other.label: Sequencer
other.value: Illumina HiSeq 2000 / HiSeq 2500, 100-bp paired-end, cluster density 75–85% of normal

data_processing.summary: Paired-end bisulfite reads are quality/adapter-trimmed with RRBS-aware settings, aligned to an in-silico bisulfite-converted reference with Bismark/Bowtie1, and per-cytosine methylation is called as reported-C / (reported-C + reported-T); in single cells each CpG (>=3x) is digitized to fully methylated (>=90%->100%) or unmethylated (<=10%->0%), with non-conversion estimated from the unmethylated lambda-DNA spike-in.
data_processing.statistical_model: per-CpG methylation fraction = reported C / (reported C + T); single-cell calls digitized to 0% (<=10%) or 100% (>=90%), intermediate 10-90% discarded; non-conversion rate estimated from unmethylated lambda-DNA spike-in
